payload.description:
# Distributed budget infrastructure

## Purpose

Provide hierarchical, divisible, ambient budgets with distributed propagation through the existing task-dispatch system. Catches every category of cost runaway (vertical recursion / fork bomb, horizontal fan-out, pathological retry, abandoned runs, distributed amplification) without preventing legitimate scaled work.

## Why critical (P0)

The user has experienced cost runaways from successful systems whose scaling cost was underestimated. With dispatched operations (`plexus.evaluation.run`) exposed in v1, distributed budget propagation is not optional — without it, an LLM-orchestrated evaluation could silently spend unbounded money in remote workers while the parent's accounting shows nothing.

## Budget categories

A `Budget` value tracks all of:
- `usd` — money on LLM API + managed services (extends existing `plexus/scores/core/CostAccumulator.py`)
- `wallclock` — real-time elapsed (HITL pauses don't tick; LLM calls do)
- `tokens` — input + output token totals
- `llm_calls` — count, catches retry loops where each call is cheap
- `tool_calls` — count, catches fan-out without batching
- `depth` — recursion depth across `execute_tactus` and dispatched workers
- `workers` — count of active + lifetime spawned workers, catches fork-bomb shapes

## Hierarchical / divisible semantics

- Every `execute_tactus` call has a top-level budget envelope with conservative defaults (e.g., $0.25 / 60s / depth 3).
- Workers spawned from inside it get sub-budgets carved out of the parent.
- Parent's remaining envelope shrinks immediately on spawn.
- Children cannot exceed their carved budget.
- `plexus.budget.with({ usd = 0.50 }, function() ... end)` for explicit sub-scopes; ambient debit otherwise.
- Spawning a Worker MUST declare its budget explicitly — no implicit recursion budget. Structurally prevents fork bombs.

## Distributed propagation

When a Plexus operation (e.g., `plexus.evaluation.run`) dispatches work to remote Celery workers (per `.cursor/rules/task-dispatch-system.mdc`):
- The `Budget` value serializes into the dispatch payload.
- Each remote worker debits cost-incurring operations from its allocation locally.
- Cost events stream back to the parent in near-real-time (channel TBD: GraphQL subscription / SQS / Redis / S3 logs — investigate during implementation).
- Parent runtime aggregates incoming events and updates its budget continuously.
- If a remote worker exhausts its allocation, it halts gracefully with partial results.
- If the parent's budget hits, parent signals workers to cancel.

## Failure semantics

- 80% of any cap → soft signal to the agent (system message: "you are 80% through your token budget, finish up").
- 100% → hard kill with structured error returned to caller.
- Optional `Human.approve`-to-extend if the procedure declares it.
- HITL pauses suspend wallclock and money clocks (they don't tick during human waits) but the HITL pause itself has its own cap (e.g., 24h).

## Account-level guardrails (separate from per-call budgets)

- Per-account daily / monthly hard cap. Refused at MCP entry point.
- Account-level rate limiter on suspicious patterns.
- Default `execute_tactus` budget is small so a forgetful caller cannot accidentally spend $10.

## Bypass prevention

- Single accounting choke point — every cost-incurring operation goes through one function. Bypassing is not a code-style issue but an architectural error caught by static analysis or runtime asserts in dev mode.
- Account-level cap independent of per-call budget so an agent cannot circumvent by making N small calls.

## Estimate

2–3 weeks of focused work, the dominant cost in the initiative. Distributed-systems plumbing (cost event channel, reconciliation loop, cancellation propagation) is the bulk; budget value type and hierarchical math are the easy parts.

## Dependencies

Requires understanding of existing task-dispatch system. The first sub-task should be a focused exploration of `.cursor/rules/task-dispatch-system.mdc` and the actual dispatch code paths to confirm what serialization, return-channel, and cancellation primitives exist today.
payload.title: Distributed budget infrastructure
